hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.label: Hilbert annihilator range criterion
hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.reason: For a densely defined closed trace map R between Hilbert spaces, closure Ran(R^*)=(ker R)^perp.  Hence E_active factors through the interval trace iff ker R is annihilated by E_active.

finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.label: finite active block factorization
finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.reason: Current Galerkin active blocks satisfy E_active=C_sample R_global to roundoff.  This is finite evidence for, not proof of, the continuum annihilator condition.

compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.label: compactness contradiction reduction
compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.reason: Failure of ker R subset ker E_active is equivalent to an A-normalized sequence with R f_n -> 0 and active source bounded below.  The high-block elliptic/Mosco theorem is the compactness mechanism needed to pass such a sequence to a continuum limit.

oldContinuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilit.label: continuum active trace range compatibility
oldContinuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilit.reason: The closed-trace active unique-continuation theorem proves ker R subset ker E_active.  The Hilbert annihilator criterion then gives E_active in closure Ran(R^*).

synchronizedEndpointKrawczykInputStatus.label: synchronized endpoint Krawczyk input
synchronizedEndpointKrawczykInputStatus.reason: Imported from the synchronized 200-point coefficient input certificate.  It proves the finite endpoint map stays inside the persistent active Pluecker chart.

endpointGreenBvpSolvabilityStatus.label: endpoint Green BVP solvable for active source rows
endpointGreenBvpSolvabilityStatus.reason: Full active endpoint row rank removes every Fredholm compatibility obstruction, so the endpoint free vector z can be chosen for every active source row.

closedTraceActiveAnnihilationStatus.label: closed trace annihilates active source
closedTraceActiveAnnihilationStatus.reason: With the endpoint Green BVP solvable, the Volterra/Sturm Green representation has only the interval trace term.  If Lambda_a(f)=0 on I, that term vanishes and E_active f=0.

activeRangeInclusionStatus.label: E_active in closure Range(R_global^*)
activeRangeInclusionStatus.reason: By the Hilbert annihilator criterion, ker R_global subset ker E_active is equivalent to E_active lying in closure Range(R_global^*).

sourceInactiveTailDominationStatus.label: source-inactive min-max tail domination
sourceInactiveTailDominationStatus.reason: Imported min-max theorem gives ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= epsilon_delta <A f,f> in the certified normalized source model, and epsilon_delta is below the finite low/mid Schur budget.

certifiedModelCombinedSourceBoundStatus.label: active range plus inactive tail in certified source model
certifiedModelCombinedSourceBoundStatus.reason: On ker R_global, the active source component vanishes; the inactive component is bounded by epsilon_delta and absorbed by the finite low/mid Schur block.

fullContinuumCombinedSourceBoundStatus.label: full continuum active range plus inactive tail
fullContinuumCombinedSourceBoundStatus.reason: Closed: active range inclusion is proved by the synchronized endpoint Green theorem, and the inactive-tail theorem imports the Galerkin-to-continuum high-block exhaustion passage.

proofChain.statement: synchronized endpoint Krawczyk input closed implies the active endpoint map has full row rank.
full active endpoint row rank implies the endpoint Green BVP is solvable for every active source row.
the solved Green representation gives R_global f=0 implies E_active f=0.
ker R_global subset ker E_active implies E_active in closure Range(R_global^*).
source-inactive min-max tail gives ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= epsilon_delta <A f,f>.

formalConclusion: For f in ker R_global, P_active Ehat f=0 and ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= 1.345452638275e-03 <A f,f> in the certified normalized source model. The finite low/mid Schur budget is 5.730309711104e-03, leaving slack 4.384857072828e-03.
